mission.title: opentofu: Unlocking Terraform's S3 Backend: Going DynamoDB-Free
mission.description: Unlocking Terraform's S3 Backend: Going DynamoDB-Free. Requested by 108+ users.

steps.title: Check current opentofu deployment
steps.description:
Verify your opentofu version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentofu
helm list -n opentofu 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working opentofu installation.

steps.title: Review opentofu configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant opentofu configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentofu
kubectl get configmap -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opentofu
```
This RFC Propose  a significant enhancement to terraform's S3 backend configuration. The objective is to provide a DynamoDB-free alternative for state file locking, making our infrastructure management more flexible and cost-efficient.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Unlocking Terraform's S3 Backend: Going DynamoDB-Free
steps.description:
RFC : https://github.com/opentofu/opentofu/issues/599
```yaml
terraform {
    backend "s3" {
      bucket = "terraform-backend-ravi"
      region = "ap-south-1"
      skip_region_validation = true
      skip_credentials_validation = true
      force_path_style = true
      key = "terraform.tfstate"
      lock_storage_type = "S3Bucket"
      access_key = "<access_key>"
      secret_key = "<Secrete_Key>"
    }
}
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentofu
kubectl get events -n opentofu --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Unlocking Terraform's S3 Backend: Going DynamoDB-Free" is functioning correctly.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with opentofu installed or the issue environment reproducible.
